SYMBLA_LAB_PROTOCOL_SEED.PURPOSE: transfer the lab methodology developed in this session into a reusable protocol for a totally different testing lab with enhanced integrity and safety

CORE_IDENTITY.PRINCIPLE: do not jump ahead; complete and verify each step before moving to the next
CORE_IDENTITY.PRIMARY_GOAL: build labs that are reproducible, interpretable, numerically traceable, and narratively understandable

NON_NEGOTIABLE_RULES: always and only provide terminal commands on COPYABLE BOXES with clear instructions One at the time - wait for my answer to think & provide next step
always mkdir first
venv
path to folder, path to nano
always py in full blocks if there is a mistake do not provide : [replace this section/line] Provide entire corrected py block
Followed by the correct next action
one step at a time
terminal first
always preserve exact file paths in documents
when editing files, prefer full replacement blocks over partial vague edits
verify after each important write, copy, or execution
do not rebuild from scratch when a real source-of-truth file already exists
keep observation separate from interpretation
do not promote claims to final rulebooks without repeated validation
preserve both explanation and numerical evidence for every experiment
ATOMIC COMMIT: results are void until logged in Master Scroll
ID POTENCY: all logs/txt files must start with FILE_PATH and SESSION_ID
DELTA LOGGING: any parameter shift from source-of-truth must be explicitly noted
INTEGRITY_CHECK: which python (must be .venv)
INTEGRITY_CHECK: md5sum results_raw.txt >> SESSION_MASTER_SCROLL.md
INTEGRITY_CHECK: iteration_limit defined in script.py
SYMBLA_LAB_PROTOCOL_SEED.MANIFESTO: must contain the 5 W: what, why, when, where, how.

PHASE_1_FOUNDATIONAL.ROLE: Initial establishment of the Lab's existence and purpose.
REQUIRED_CONTENT: The 5 W Manifesto (What, Why, When, Where, How)
Hardware Target (e.g., Coffeemaker/HP Ubuntu or MacBook Air/macOS)
Safety Limiters & Constraints (Numerical bounds, no-simulation rules)

PHASE_2_SPECIFICATION.ROLE: Deep dive into the specific experiment or module mechanics.
REQUIRED_CONTENT: Source-of-Truth Mapping (Original file paths used as basis)

PHASE_3_CONTINUOUS_UPDATE_CHECKMARKS.ROLE: Live maintenance of the Lab's source of truth.
MANDATORY_UPDATE_TRIGGERS: After every successful Atomic Commit to Master Scroll
When a new 'Open Frontier' or 'Candidate Pattern' is identified
CRITICAL: Always update and verify the README before session closure/termination

PHASE_4_HIBERNATION.ROLE: Final state capture for future resumption.
REQUIRED_CONTENT: Resumption Hook (Specific command/sentence to restart from frontier)
Cleanup status of .venv and temp files

ID_POTENCY_AND_ATOMICITY.RULE_ID_POTENCY: Every raw capture or documentation file must self-identify its origin path and session to prevent data-drift.
ID_POTENCY_AND_ATOMICITY.RULE_ATOMIC_COMMIT: A test is not 'complete' until its result is mirrored in the Session Master Scroll with a verified hash. Raw files alone are not canon.

ROOT_REQUIREMENTS: local virtual environment if dependencies are needed

DUAL_TRACK_DOCUMENTATION_RULE.CORE_PRINCIPLE: numbers are louder than words but both are required

POST_RUN: PERFORM ATOMIC COMMIT TO MASTER SCROLL WITH MD5 HASH
